behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file (components/cascader/style/columns.ts) and describes required CSS properties (minWidth: 0, overflow: hidden, white-space: nowrap, text-overflow: ellipsis) on the content element, which matches what tests assert.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the problem and principles (minWidth: 0, properties on text element not parent) without stating exact code changes or diff.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert specifically on the nested structure '&-menu' > '&-item' > '&-content' with exact property values (contentBlock.overflow === 'hidden', whiteSpace === 'nowrap', textOverflow === 'ellipsis', minWidth === 0); an alternative fix using textEllipsis spread or different selector naming wouldn't satisfy the literal key check, though the instruction's principles do constrain this closely.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests bundle the TS via esbuild and execute getColumnsStyle with a mock token, inspecting the returned CSS object structure.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_css_ellipsis_behavior requires actual property values on &-content block; a stub wouldn't satisfy it.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests check minWidth: 0 and textEllipsis on content element, matching instruction's stated requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with clear section headers (Fail-to-Pass, Pass-to-Pass) and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces CSS-in-JS style output, not user-facing structured data; schema inspected via tests.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent (components/cascader/style/columns.ts matches across instruction, tests, and solution).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file but not the specific line or internal structure; localization within the file remains the agent's task.
